details: The “Supply of Agriculture Machineries Scheme” was launched by the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya for the distribution and sale of Agriculture Machineries such as Power Tillers, etc. to farmers at a subsidized rate.
benefits: Subsidy on Power tillers, Reapers, Harvesters, etc.
Type of Assistance and Entitlement (If Any):
eligibility: The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
The applicant should be a bonafide farmer engaged in agriculture.

offline: The eligible bonafide farmer can apply/write an application on plain paper.
Along with the application, attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application along with the documents at the nearest office of the Agriculture Development Officer of a CD Block/Agriculture Circle.
The bonafide farmers shall be selected on a first come first serve basis.
District Agriculture Officers of the concerned districts
Sub-divisional Officers of the concerned sub-divisions
Agriculture Development Officers of the concerned Community and Rural Development Blocks
Assistant Agriculture Engineer (Mechanical) of the concerned districts

documents_required: Documents proof of being a farmer

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of the scheme is to distribute and sale of Agricultural machinery such as Power Tillers, etc. to farmers at a subsidized rate.

faqs.question: Which department has launched this scheme?
faqs.answer: Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The bonafide farmers of Meghalaya engaged in agriculture are eligible to apply under the scheme.

faqs.question: What is the pattern of assistance?
faqs.answer: Subsidy on Power tillers, Reapers, Harvesters etc.

faqs.question: What is the type of assistance/service?
How to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The bonafide farmers can apply in plain paper through the nearest Agriculture Development Officer of a CD Block/ Agriculture Circle.

faqs.question: What is the beneficiaries’ selection procedure?
faqs.answer: The bonafide farmers shall be selected on a first come first served basis.

faqs.question: Whom to contact?
faqs.answer: 1. District Agriculture Officers of the concerned districts. 2. Sub-divisional Officers of the concerned sub-divisions. 3. Agriculture Development Officers of the concerned Community and Rural Development Blocks. 4. Assistant Agriculture Engineer (Mechanical) of the concerned districts.
